description: The Stopwatch node keeps track of time when running. Akin to a real stopwatch, it allows one to start time, stop time, and reset time.

fields.Start: Starts the stopwatch, putting it in the running state.
fields.Stop: Stops the stopwatch, putting it in the stopped state.
fields.Reset: Resets the stopwatch time. Does not affect the state of the stopwatch.
fields.OnStarted: Sends an impulse when ``Start`` is impulsed. Is not affected by the state of the stopwatch.
fields.OnStopped: Sends an impulse when ``Stop`` is impulsed. Is not affected by the state of the stopwatch.
fields.OnReset: Sends an impulse when ``Reset`` is impulsed. Is not affected by the state of the stopwatch.
fields.Time:
The time in seconds stored by the stopwatch. This value increments when in the running state and freezes when in the stopped state. If ``Reset`` is pulsed, this output is set to ``0``, but continues the same behavior of the state the watch is in.

Sometimes, it may be desirable to obtain a TimeSpan type from this value. To do so, use a TimeSpan From Seconds node.
fields.IsRunning: ``True`` if the stopwatch is in a running state, ``False`` otherwise.
